sections.paragraph:
ἀποτίκτω 1 aor. pass. ptc. ἀποτεχθέντες 4 Macc 13:21 (s. τίκτω; Pla., Plut.; Artem. 1, 16; 4 Macc 13:21; 14:16; Philo, Virt. 139, Aet. M. 60; Ath. 22, 4; 27, 1) bring to birth τὰ ἀποτικτόμενα children born after a full-term pregnancy (in contrast to premature births or abortions) ApcPt Fgm. 3 p. 12, 31.—DELG s.v. τίκτω.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 124
